Document:
# Solarian Directives (SD)
[color=#999999][italic]"The Triad sector brings us many opportunities like growth, profit, and most importantly; research."[/italic][/color] [color=#999999][italic]- Lutz Mueller, Terralith R&M Executive Specialist[/italic][/color]
Solarian Directives is an association of a number of factions and companies that work together to gain profit and influence on other sectors in the name of Sol. They are the main driver for most of Sol's technological advancement, and the reason why Sol's influence is very prominent all throughout the galaxy. There are more than one Solarian Directive active at a time, they are the association that was formed by the government of Sol, the [color=lightblue]Federated Solarian Republic (FSR)[/color] and were sent out into the expanse of space to claim territory, gather research, or procure materials or perhaps even invent new ones. Every Solarian Directive has their own callsign that the FSR designates, but for those that are outside the Sol system, they are known as the Solarian Directive.
The Solarian Directives' use of PMC groups to forcefully take over sectors and planets have caused an uproar within the FSR, many citizens criticizing the government for allowing the SD to do such things, though the Republic defends them saying what they do is necessary to ensure the prosperity of the Sol system and its controlled territories. Very little action is taken against the SD from the FSR due to corporate favoritism in the higher government and the great amount of expansion the SD has brought to the Republic.
## Local Solarian Directive Force Their designation is [color=lightgreen]Directive Jupiter[/color], but this is mostly unknown to non-Solarians, who refer to them as "The Solarians", "Solarian Directive", or "SD" by the locals of Triad and the [textlink="Coalition" link="ImperialCoalition"].
They are comprised of three groups:
### Terralith Research & Manufacturing This is the main company known to lead the directive, being a large manufacturer of cutting-edge weaponry in the Sol system, such as plasma weapons, laser weapons, and shielding. They are known to utilize PMCs to secure their sites and guard their missions, such as Blue Diamond PMCs. Their heavy losses in the Triad sector from the fifty-year war has invigorated them to begin operations in the sector with their directive, Terralith itself being particularly interested in the Monolithic activities of the sector. They were the controllers of the automated drone swarms used to block the farther reaches of the sector, but after the fifty-year war Terralith lost control of these drone swarms due to the damage from electronics they suffered from years worth of solar flares and ion storms. The drones now patrol the outer ring, attacking any vessel in sight, even Solarian Directive ones.
### Blue Diamond Military Contracting The force that carries out the will of the directive through via arms. They have had connections with Terralith for many years, serving a small fleet during the fifty-year war. Blue Diamond is currently hired by Terralith and are the primary acting force when it comes to combat activities and the armed takeover of points of interest in the Triad Sector, and in other sectors. Being a much smaller military force than the other groups that Terralith hired in the fifty-year war, Blue Diamond makes up for their small size by having a plethora, advanced weapons, equipment, and of course skills.
### Helios Power Solutions Inc. Helios Power Solutions Inc. is an energy providing and logistics company. They ensure fuel and energy is properly supplied to SD fleets and ensures cargo is properly delivered to their facilities. Helios is primarily interested in mining operations in the Triad sector, especially the large amounts of bluespace ore that they can use to continue their research on bluespace reactors. Helios also provides mining and salvage vessels for the local SD force to ensure that materials can be procured during operations.
### The Directive's Relations They have hostile relations with the [textlink="TIC" link="ImperialCoalition"] and [textlink="TDF" link="TriadDefenseForce"] due to their past conflicts in the 50-year war and are in a cold-war relationship with the [textlink="TIC" link="ImperialCoalition"], the directive actively surveying both the Coalition's movements and the activities of the TDF.
